Army's xTech NatSec hackathon names winners

By Brandi Vincent

The U.S. Army FUZE xTech Program announced the winners of the third annual National Security Hackathon at the Cerebral Valley venue in San Francisco. Five teams took home a share of the $50,000 prize pool after two days of building prototypes.

First place went to Hadrian Automation, with their precision manufacturing telemetry tool. Second place was awarded to a stealth team operating as "Project Ironhide," whose founders declined to be named on the record but confirmed they are pre-incorporation.

Third place went to Vannevar Labs for their open-source intelligence prototype, and fourth to Saronic Technologies, demoing a maritime autonomy concept. Fifth place was Performance Drone Works, which presented a low-cost loitering munition control loop.

Other notable demos came from Shield AI, Skydio, EpiSci, and Mach Industries, none of which placed in the top five but were widely recognized on the floor. Judges included representatives from Shield Capital, In-Q-Tel, and DCVC. Mentors throughout the weekend included engineers from OpenAI, Scale AI, and Armada AI.

The Cerebral Valley team confirmed that finalists will be invited to pursue follow-on OT prototyping contracts under 10 U.S.C. § 4022.
